column3: "Avocado boosts immunity" refers to its ability to improve one's body's natural defense system, helping fight off diseases and infections more effectively.
"Avocado reduces inflammation" refers to its ability to decrease swelling and pain due to bodily responses or conditions, especially related to digestion.

"Avocado lowers blood sugar" refers to its ability to regulate insulin levels in the body which consequently helps control 
         and maintain a stable balance of sugar or glucose in the blood.
"Avocado improves heart health" refers to its ability to enhance cholesterol levels and reduce blood pressure.
"Avocado promotes digestion" refers to its ability to ease bowel movements and enhance nutrient absorption during the process of breaking down food.

"Avocado reduces stress" refers to its ability to improve mental health by providing essential nutrients such as vitamins B and E, monounsaturated fats, and potassium, which help regulate hormones, ease anxiety, and promote relaxation.
"Avocado aids sleep" refers to its potential beneficial effect on an individual's ability to fall asleep faster or have better quality sleep due to certain nutrients it contains.
"Avocado relieves headaches" refers to its ability to reduce pain caused by headache, thanks to its nutrients and healthy fats.
"Avocado enhances cognition" refers to its ability to boost brain function, memory, and concentration.
"Avocado boosts energy levels" refers to its nutritional content that provides essential vitamins and minerals which enhance our body's energy efficiency.
